title: scabiosa atropurpurea subsp. thysdrusiana
intro: Scabiosa atropurpurea subsp. thysdrusiana, also known as a subspecies of the Scabiosa atropurpurea plant, is a flowering plant in the borage family (Boraginaceae). It is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region, known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional folk medicine. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, fever, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which also contribute to its health benefits such as reducing pain and promoting wound healing. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to extract its medicinal properties.
